players.name: Latrell Mitchell
players.note: Latrell Mitchell's run of centuries came to an end overnight with the star centre clearly hindered by a back complaint. The Rabbitohs' centre is firmly on the sell list given he misses at least five of the next seven weeks with Origin and byes. Mitchell will be difficult to trade back in post-Origin, however his value will drop to assist in this.

players.name: Nathan Cleary
players.note: A dour team performance, with Cleary's only joy coming off the back of two line breaks. The automatic captain fell to an emotional Cowboys side. Next up is the Broncos in round 20.

players.name: Herbie Farnworth
players.note: Elite performance in a tough match up by Herbie Farnworth. 8th try of the season yielded his fourth 100-point score of the season. Falls short of VC. Mouth-watering match up next week against the Knights.

players.name: Nicholas Hynes
players.note: The Sharks' spine picked a part a staggered Bulldogs' defence on more than one occasion. Hynes crossed for his fourth try of the season, beating his breakeven. The Sharks' halfback will be one of the most valuable players after the round 12. Must-bye by round 14 against the Dragons.

players.name: Terrell May
players.note: 80 minutes and 80+ in base for the second week in a row. Terrell May was a low-risk captaincy option with the Tigers' devoid of forwards. Expect May to maintain his big-minute role with only Alex Twal a chance to return from injury.

players.name: Kalyn Ponga
players.note: Only the weather can hold Ponga back in this one. The Tigers' have leaked 89-points on average to the last four fullbacks.

players.name: Thomas Jenkins
players.note: A lone bright spot for the Panthers as Jenkins' crossed for his 10th try double of the season. Jenkins is a buy heading into round 18. Run home appeal remains high.

players.name: Sua Fa'alogo
players.note: Until last week, the Sea Eagles had been stout against opposing fullbacks. Connor Tracey's score of 79 was the second highest score by a fullback this season. This bodes well for Fa'alogo who is coming off one of his best games of the season.

players.name: Haumole Olakau'atu
players.note: Fifth try double of his career. An 80-point blitz in the opening two minutes ensured Olakau'atu would beat his breakeven. The performance was enough to earn an Origin recall, however if he doesn't, owners will get an extra gun in round 18.

players.name: Alex Twal
players.note: A late knee injury soured what was a quality performance returning from injury. Twal is expected to be sidelined short-term. Owners who can over Twal could hold given the Tigers' play the next two months.

players.name: Hudson Young
players.note: Classy bounce-back for Hudson Young who scored his fourth try of the season. A typical showing with Young popping up all over the field. Once Origin is finished, the Raiders' forward is a must-have for Supercoach. A player to break the bank for.

players.name: Adam Doueihi
players.note: A successful return from injury as Adam Doueihi sets up Tony Sukkar's first career try. At his current price, and the state of the Tigers attack, coaches can wait on Doueihi short-term.

players.name: Jamayne Isaako
players.note: Two tries by Isaako who profited against a new Warriors left-edge defence. Ninth consecutive match with 12-points or more. Set and forget against the Knights next week.

players.name: Jackson Ford
players.note: 80-minutes for Ford, exactly what owners hoped in a Warriors' forward pack missing James Fisher-Harris and Mitch Barnett. Ford has been able to maintain a big-minute role with a strong work-rate for long periods this season. Until this changes, he is a season keeper.

players.name: Te Maire Martin
players.note: Te Maire Martin maintains his strong form in top 4 match up. A try save by Hamiso Tabuai-Fidow applied the clamps to Martin's scoring this week, despite featuring elsewhere. Unless owners are upgrading to Nicho Hynes, it's best to hold Martin through the bye.

players.name: Dylan Lucas
players.note: Seventh try in six matches. Despite the tough conditions, Lucas sparked the Knights attack with their first points this afternoon. If there was any doubt that Lucas was a top 2RF for the run home, just check out his most recent scores. Misses Origin selection. Premium buy from round 18.

players.name: Simi Sasagi
players.note: A huge return from injury with Sasagi running 24 times from right center. His attacking upside takes a hit with Ethan Strange moving to the left. If you own Sasagi, you're holding. If you don't, he's in your watch list.

players.name: Alex Johnston
players.note: A rare game where Alex Johnston failed to score - in fact the Rabbitohs provided him with zero opportunities in attacking positions. Johnston is a watch as we end the Origin period. His value will receive a boost with Latrell Mitchell returns.

players.name: Dylan Edwards
players.note: Expect Dylan Edwards' scoring patterns to continue against the Cowboys on Saturday. His opponent have conceded 78-points on average to the last four fullbacks.

players.name: James Tedesco
players.note: Tedesco found most of his success playing through the middle third, notching up his 10th try assist of the season. Despite Tedesco's sharp dip in price in recent times, we've seen enough from the Roosters' fullback to hold for the remainder of the season.

players.name: Thomas Dearden
players.note: A lower leg injury soured an outstanding round 10 performance that included four try assists. It's a mark Deaden has reached just three times in his career. The Cowboys and Billy Slater face a nervous wait on a clearly hobbled halfback.

players.name: Jack Bostock
players.note: A classy bounce back effort by Jack Bostock who produced his sixth try assist of the season. He continues to be the Dolphins' go-to on high kicks, maintaining solid SC appeal. Owners will want to hold until at least the Dolphins final bye in round 22.

players.name: Tevita Naufahu
players.note: Huge in the absence of Selwyn Cobbo. Naufahu Whyte has shown plenty of speed and class on the Dolphins' wing. A small taste into things to come in 2027, Nahfahu could be a super pod for the run home, if the Dolphins' loose 1-2 backs.

players.name: Tallis Duncan
players.note: Right centre masterclass as Tallis Duncan produces two second half tries to crush his breakeven. With dual CTW status set to be added, Duncan is a strong hold for the remainder of the season.

players.name: Payne Haas
players.note: 80-minutes for Haas who had to fill the void left by a concussed Xavier Willison. Owners who brought in Haas got just-rewards given his current price, and the fact he will miss the next two weeks through Origin and a bye. Come round 20, Haas is a must-have at an affordable price. Owners can bank that trade somewhere else post-Origin.

players.name: Scott Drinkwater
players.note: The Panthers have allowed 58-points on average to the last four opposing fullbacks. Drinkwater is a 50/50 chance to bottom out in price in round 17. Watch.

players.name: Jayden Campbell
players.note: A spluttering Titans' attack and error count kept the lid of Jayden Campbell's touches in the red zone. First game where Campbell has missed three goals and not kicked one. He will quickly peak in price heading into the Titans round 18 bye. Dual status from next week will keep Campbell as a top 5/8 or HLF option for the run home.

players.name: Harry Grant
players.note: Two assists sending Stefano and Loiero over from short range ensured Grant's scoring trends continue. Harry Grant will be sitting at his highest price since late 2023. Hold through the Origin period.

players.name: Latrell Siegwalt
players.note: Perfect with the boot as Latrell sets up two tries for Alex Johnston. The popular replacement for Latrell Mitchell came through in the clutch in the second major bye week. With the Bunnies on a bye in round 16, Mitchell's return from injury could be as soon as round 17. Siegwalt owners will want to hold as long as possible.

players.name: Samuela Fainu
players.note: A late ankle injury soured another promising performance which included a try assist to Taylan May. Fainu is a short-term sell.

players.name: Tanah Boyd
players.note: A classy round nine performance by Tanah Boyd who bounced back with a try assist and effective short passing game. Expect the Warriors' halfback to bottom out in price heading into his next game. Unless coaches are upgrading to a top HLF, Boyd is worth holding with decent early bye coverage.

players.name: Bradman Best
players.note: A rare game without a try assist, as Bradman Best's running game came to the fore in wet conditions in Newcastle. The Knights run home and ability with the ball make Best a top tier CTW option for the run home. His injury history is the only concern.

players.name: Ben Trbojevic
players.note: Third try in four matches. The most improved Trbojevic brother has seen a solidified scoring floor and attacking upside under coach Keiran Foran. A POD option for the run home who is healthy. Round 18 coverage.

players.name: Mark Nawaqanitawase
players.note: A night to forget for Mark Nawaqanitawase who produced his second lowest score in Roosters colours. The wet conditions and error count hampered his usual attacking flair . One poor performance is unlikely to keep him out of Origin. Next up is the Eels in round 19.

players.name: Robert Toia
players.note: POD for the run home? Robert Toia has a habit of feeding Mark Nawawanitawase, while also scoring on a semi-regular basis. Toia ranks equal 9th in base stats among CTWs. Roosters will be one of the teams coaches load up on for the run home, and Toia could be a strong play for those with or without Marky Mark.

players.name: Jacob Kiraz
players.note: A rib injury cut short a gutsy round 17 performance by Kiraz who was at his usual offloading best. The Bulldogs' bye in round 18 could be the best thing for Kiraz and his list of injuries. He will be a must-have for the run home, pending health.

players.name: Blayke Brailey
players.note: A suspected broken arm has ended Blayke Brailey's night after a promising start which included a try assist. The Sharks' hooker is now a sell.

players.name: Tolutau Koula
players.note: Outstanding display backing up from Origin as Koula laid on two try assists to Lehi Hopoate. With Turbo back in the team, Koula has plenty of upside in his scoring, despite developing a quality scoring floor this season.

players.name: Toby Couchman
players.note: Strong base and PPM by Toby Couchman has done enough for his price to bottom out heading into round 18. Injuries to popular options in Jackson Ford, KPP and Ativalu Lisati open the door for Couchman to be a mass addition next week. Current owners are sitting pretty.

players.name: Greg Marzhew
players.note: An unthinkable three-game stretch with no tries has sent Marzhew's price spiralling heading into the final major bye. It will provide an opportunity for non-owners to get on at an affordable price over the next fortnight. Current owners should be holding. It will turn. The Knights' run home will provide plenty of opportunities for Marzhew.

players.name: Keano Kini
players.note: The Titans' attack went through Kini in wet conditions overnight. It was a classy output in wet conditions, with the Titans' fullback relying on his offloading game through the middle. Owners could hold through the bye.

players.name: Ryan Couchman
players.note: Josh Kerr was effectively a direct swap for Ryan Couchman who held down the middle for the opening 59-minutes of the match. Strong play heading into round 18.

players.name: Lachlan Galvin
players.note: Lachlan Galvin reverted to his running ways, crossing for an early try against the Cowboys. In a Bulldogs' attack that is lacking confidence, Galvin has been one of the lone bright spots. With Origin fast approaching, loading up on 2-3 Bulldogs could be handy for the major byes. Galvin would be a POD purchase.

players.name: Jake Averillo
players.note: A late try assist helped Jake Averillo match a positive match up the Sea Eagles. With all the attention on Herbie Farnworth, Averillo and his increased attacking opportunities have gone widely unnoticed. POD play at a premium price.

players.name: Isaiah Papali'i
players.note: A late try assist to Tom Jenkins was the cherry on top following a huge base stat effort against the Dolphins. Isaiah Papali'i has flashed several times this season as a big-minute, strong work-rate forward on the edge. This was just another example. While not a buy, last night's performance is a welcome rebound after the last fortnight.

players.name: Heilum Luki
players.note: Second consecutive match with 60+ in base, fifth time this season. Heilum Luki's scoring patterns remain steady without Tom Dearden inside hiim. Despite no round 15/18 bye coverage, Luki is worth holding barring injury.

players.name: Jake Clifford
players.note: One of the top 5/8 options available, Jake Clifford runs into a tough test against a Bunnies' side who have held the last four right side playmakers to 51 points on average.

players.name: Tom Trbojevic
players.note: With the Sea Eagles up big, Tom Trbojevic got an early mark. Turbo will bottom out in price heading into round 18. The price and appeal is there. Injuries are a concern. The Sea Eagles' form is a reason for upside. Buy.

players.name: Braidon Burns
players.note: A consolation try will ensure Braidon Burns will bottom out heading into round nine. The Cowboys' winger has proved efficient this season crossing for six tries on a favourable right edge. Coaches looking for a slight under-priced CTW with good early bye coverage, look here. POD appeal.

players.name: Casey McLean
players.note: An ankle injury cut short Casey McLean's afternoon after a promising start. While he could of returned, he remained on the sidelines with the Panthers' up big.

players.name: Kulikefu Finefeuiaki
players.note: Starting from the bench, Finekeuiaki made little impact on the right edge with the Dolphins' finding plenty of success on the left edge. Now Finefeuiaki has dipped below 600k, the value isn't quite there to trade out. Hold. Look for a rebound after the Origin period.

players.name: Isaah Yeo
players.note: 59-minutes for Yeo in round 17. The Panthers' lock has slowed in recent weeks through a middle forward rotation with Jack Cogger, and limited attacking stats. Yeo could be upgraded in preparation for the run home.

players.name: Kai Pearce-Paul
players.note: An early pec injury has ended Kai Pearce-Paul's afternoon. With coaches burning though trades at this time of the season, KPP is now a sell.

players.name: Sam Walker
players.note: Big-game Sam Walker returned, accumulating points in a wet outing at Suncorp Stadium overnight. Walker has trumped DCE as the primary playmaker, and is set to rise in price quite quickly once again. Post-Origin, the Roosters' halfback is a POD halfback option and a foil to owners with Nicho Hynes.

players.name: Jethro Rinakama
players.note: First game of the season on the Bulldogs' left wing, Rinakama crosses for two tries. Job security is solid with Connor Tracey pushing Jacob Kiraz to fullback. Watch.

players.name: Patrick Carrigan
players.note: An ankle injury hindered Patrick Carrigan in backing up from Origin. The Broncos' lock played 42-minutes before leaving the field. While only minor, Carrigan will miss a minimum of four games over the next six weeks. Sell.

players.name: Clayton Faulalo
players.note: An early sin bin caps Faulalo's scoring, despite a second half try. The Manly backup fullback has now peaked in price, and with Tom Trbojevic set to return from injury following the Sea Eagles' round 15 bye, now is the now the perfect time to sell Faulalo.

players.name: Charnze Nicoll-Klokstad
players.note: Fifth try on the left wing in two matches. CNK misses out on dual status which will see his ownership remain minute for the run home. Has value in draft.

players.name: Hamiso Tabuai-Fidow
players.note: Three try involvements in the space of 15-minutes to post his first 100-point effort of the season. The Dolphins' fullback remains a strong POD option given his attacking upside. Base numbers are poor.

players.name: Kayal Iro
players.note: A successful return from injury as KL Iro produced his second try assist of the season. Given his price and breakeven, Iro is a tough trade in for non-owners searching for round 15 coverage. Current owners could have a top CTW all to themselves in a eye-catching match up against the Warriors.

players.name: Hamish Stewart
players.note: Third consecutive match with 60+ in base stats. We flagged his attacking credentials earlier in the season - the last two matches, Stewart has managed three offloads. While still short of the top 2RF options, if he can continue to accumulate attacking stats, Stewart could prove valuable during the run home. Straight forward swap for Lisati.

players.name: Ativalu Lisati
players.note: An ankle injury ended Lisati's night. He was able to walk from the field albeit gingerly. He shapes are a sell heading into round 18.

players.name: Dominic Young
players.note: The Knights' attack has been effective when hitting the right edge, albeit less than the left side. Young has flashed top tier CTW upside. Owners will want to hold short-term and play the match ups up until the H2H finals.

players.name: Jordan Riki
players.note: Mr consistent on the Broncos' right edge. Jordan Riki's health and scoring floor could prove pivotal heading into the Origin period. One to consider for teams struggling to cop with injuries and peaking 2RFs.

players.name: Jahrome Hughes
players.note: Jahrome Hughes was one of the Storm's best in an attacking night out against the Knights in round 14. His base output is his second lowest in Storm colours, and lowest in an 80-minute performance. A volatile scoring range is there, however Hughes' playmaking consistency hides this issue.

players.name: David Fifita
players.note: 80-minutes on the edge and a late try assist to Tallis Duncan. David Fifita is slowly bouncing back. Owners could hold for the rest of the season given his attacking upside. Fifita has added round 18 appeal, especially sub-600k.

players.name: Ronaldo Mulitalo
players.note: With no shortage of attacking opportunities, Ronaldo Mulitalo posted his second highest SC score against the Dragons on Sunday afternoon. We've seen his scoring range in three games this season. He will be a welcome, albeit risky addition for the remainder of the Origin period.

players.name: Murray Taulagi
players.note: Sixth try of the season as Murray Taulagi capitalises on the edge of the Cowboys stellar left edge attack. Both he and Jaxon Purdue are forming a lethal combo. A tough one to consider until after Origin.

players.name: Siua Wong
players.note: 66-points in base as Siua Wong proves a solid POD during round 15. Inconsistent attacking numbers will limit his run home value, however his base scoring in unquestioned.

players.name: Cameron Munster
players.note: The Storm attack fell flat, playing from behind against the Sea Eagles overnight. Munster struggled to ice the very few opportunities that presented themselves. Next up is the Titans' in round 19. Expect a bounce back if he backs up from Origin.

players.name: Erin Clark
players.note: Which of the Warriors' middles fills the void left by Jackson Ford? In round 16 Mitch Barnett saw an increase in game time and output. With James Fisher-Harris still sidelined, Erin Clark is a player to watch heading into round 17.

players.name: Max King
players.note: The new scoring system suits Max King. 67-points in base stats in a 56-minute effort. With Leo Thompson sidelined, King could pop up as a FRF point of difference early on.

players.name: Connelly Lemuelu
players.note: A third consecutive match with a line break and a rare try assist. Connelly Lemuelu's upside may not be as dire as first thought. Following the Dolphins' round 13 bye, coaches could load up on fringe players such as Lemuelu.

players.name: Reuben Garrick
players.note: A HIA limited Reuben Garrick in a stellar outing against the Bulldogs. Jamal Fogarty as a result took over the goal kicking duties. Garrick is a strong buy option with round 18 coverage.

players.name: Selwyn Cobbo
players.note: After inviting the Warriors back into the game, Cobbo redeemed himself with the match-winner on Saturday afternoon. Post-Origin, Cobbo will have plenty of appeal given how well the Dolphins' attack is clicking.

players.name: Tom Chester
players.note: Tom Chester's downturn in scoring has coincided with Jaxon Purdue shifting to five-eighth (right edge). The pain could be short-lived with Tom Dearden set to return in round 19. Chester could be held and used as a 5/6th CTW for the run home.

players.name: Isaiah Iongi
players.note: Isaiah Iongi has bottomed out in price following a two try-assist effort against the Raiders overnight. Iongi is the key the Eels attack, offering round 18/19 coverage. Coaches short of a fullback could look to Iongi short-term.

players.name: Jamal Fogarty
players.note: A three-game stretch consisting of six try assists - this is the type of play we saw last season at the Raiders. Fogarty is a fraction under-priced, especially if the Sea Eagles can maintain their attacking flair under coach Kieran Foran. Solid bye schedule.

players.name: Phillip Sami
players.note: First hat-trick since 2018. Strong base and three tries resulted in Sami's second highest SC score to date. The Titans' winger has flashed SC value - he will need regular attacking stats to match his solid scoring floor. POD.

players.name: Euan Aitken
players.note: POD buy who could feature strongly in round 12. The impending return of David Fifita does cloud Aitken's role, however there is a chance he or Tallis Duncan fills a void in the centres left by Latrell Mitchell in Origin. Watch.

players.name: Braydon Trindall
players.note: The key man for the Sharks in their 10-8 win over the Warriors overnight. Braydon Trindall has shined in the absence of Nicho Hynes producing six try assists in the last four matches. Handing Trindall the keys to the Sharks' attack has greatly improved his scoring floor. His value will slip when Hynes returns. Owners can hold through to round 18 at the earliest.

players.name: Cameron Murray
players.note: The Rabbitohs' best in a beaten side, Cam Murray pushes for an Origin starting spot. The star lock has shown glimpses of matching it with the top 2RF options, often it's lack of game time limiting his consistency. Murray is set to miss at least three of the next four matches through Origin and byes. Owners could depart from next week.

players.name: Joseph Tapine
players.note: Third highest base stat output of the season. Tapine owners will want to hold for the remainder of the season with season-ending injuries to key men in Jackson Ford and Kai Pearce-Paul. It could be survival of the fittest when it come to your FRFs.

players.name: Stefano Utoikamanu
players.note: Standards by Stefano who has remained relatively constant in a Storm forward pack that have been inconsistent. Owners would love to see Storm win the possession battle, and Utoikamanu run the ball more = greater tackle busts. It's a pipe dream at the moment with the Storm scratching and clawing in every little battle. Hold.

players.name: Jacob Preston
players.note: A cruel blow for Preston owners with the star edge forward leaving the field with a suspected fractured forearm. How good is bye planning? With Preston now a sell, Kai Pearce-Paul and Joe Tapine step up as the next best options from a output and bye planning standpoint. POD options in Siua Wong, Erin Clark, Jack Williams, Corey Horsburgh and Jermaine McEwen could also do a job.

players.name: Sitili Tupouniua
players.note: Back-to-back matches with a major attacking stat. Outside of Jacob Kiraz, Tupouniua was the most influential Bulldog on the field. Second career match with two try assists. The former Rooster is a clear point of difference in the Dogs' attack. Look for him to feature over the remainder of the Origin period. Limited bye coverage.

players.name: Luke Laulilii
players.note: Straight back into the side following a two-game absence, scoring two tries overnight. While job security is questionable, Luke Laulilii remains a mid-range CTW with upside. Taylan May is the better option here, but 200k more expensive.

players.name: Beau Fermor
players.note: It's taken until round 15, but Beau Fermor has crossed for his first try of the season. We're yet to see the Jayden Campbell/Fermor combo link up, this time it was Zane Harrison sending the back rower over. If Fermor could start to jag more attacking stats, he will begin to match the top 2RF options. Watch.

players.name: Dylan Egan
players.note: First try of the season as Dylan Egan play 80-minutes on the edge despite an enthusiastic 20-minute burst by Luciano Leilua. Positive signs that Hamish Stewart was the first edge forward to be given a spell, while Egan found success on the right edge. We've touched on his scoring floor in the past, this rare attacking boost will push his price beyond 600k. Needs to find attacking consistency.

players.name: Noah Martin
players.note: An ankle injury ended Noah Martin's night shortly after halftime. The Raiders' backrower is now firmly a sell option with Zac Hosking playing 80-minutes on the right edge. He projects to shift back to the bench when Hudson Young returns.

players.name: Brian To'o
players.note: A free flowing Panthers' attack provided plenty of opportunities for the backs, including Brian To'o who crossed for his second try double of the season. While the Panthers' shape left more often, they are beginning to find balance to the benefit of Paul Alamoti and To'o. Watch.

players.name: Leo Thompson
players.note: 65-minutes and a huge work-rate. Leo Thompson posts his highest score in Bulldogs' colours, flashes upside. We've touched on this in the past, when given the opportunity, Thompson often delivers. A consistent opportunity is the key.

players.name: Dane Gagai
players.note: Highest score of the season without a try or try assist. Dane Gagai found plenty of success attacking the Dragons' left edge defence, and former Origin teammate Valentine Holmes. With round 18 looming, Gagai shapes as a risk vs reward player. A wide scoring range won't provide clarity into Gagai's projections. His combo with Dom Young is coming along nicely however.

players.name: Kitione Kautoga
players.note: Kautoga bottoms out in price after crossing for his second try of the season. The Eels attack is still a work-in-progress, however they have found success on the edges in recent weeks. Coaches looking for an affordable 80-minute 2RF with attacking upside could look here.

players.name: Kelma Tuilagi
players.note: Kelma Tuilagi and Kitone Kautoga were clear focal points on the edge for the Eels in their loss to the Knights. Both men shape as POD back rowers with a wide range of scoring possibilities given their reliance on attacking numbers. Round 15/18 coverage.

players.name: Matt Burton
players.note: Best performance in Bulldogs' colours as Burton was given plenty of time and space. Limited SC value with the Bulldogs' two upcoming byes falling on the major bye weeks. Burton needs to re-gain the goal kicking duties on a regular basis to improve his scoring floor.

players.name: Corey Horsburgh
players.note: Horsburgh's lowest output since round 6. Owners could upgrade heading into round 18 with a host of options available at FRF/2RF

players.name: James Fisher-Harris
players.note: Third time is a charm, as James Fisher-Harris scored a try on his third attempt held over the try line. With the game well in hand, JFH saw less game time. Despite this, he was still able to maintain a strong work-rate. Based on Fisher-Harris' scoring trends, he will creep towards 600k sooner rather than later.

players.name: Stephen Crichton
players.note: Second consecutive match at five-eighth with Crichton producing three try assists and his highest score of the season. The Bulldogs have found success pushing their best attacking weapon into the spine. Crichton's SC value receives a slight boost. Scoring floor is questionable.

players.name: Trai Fuller
players.note: 27 runs as Trai Fuller fills-in admirably for Hammer in round 15 Fuller has draft appeal as a back up fullback. Unless starting, he has very little classic value.

players.name: Jarome Luai
players.note: Jarome Luai backs up last week's hat-trick with a three try assist effort against the Dolphins on Saturday. He remains a short-term play with plenty of upside over the next fortnight.

players.name: Teig Wilton
players.note: Your nuts and bolts 2RF to look for over the next three weeks. Teig Wilton flashes a high scoring floor and a capable fill-in for Origin or injury-riddled teams over the second have of the bye period.

players.name: Matthew Timoko
players.note: 26 runs, the most of any game in his NRL career. Do you think Matt Timoko wants his centre spot back? He could be a rock-solid POD option in round 12. Keep tabs.

players.name: Taylan May
players.note: First game back from injury, Taylan May was the most effective Tigers' back with two assists and a try. Injury concerns are real, however May's attacking upside places him in rare company. POD option if the Tigers' can re-find their attacking mojo.

players.name: Liam Sutton
players.note: A quality one-handed offload for Murray Taulagi to score keeps Liam Sutton's scoring ticking over. The window for Tom Dearden's return opens up following the Cowboys' round 15 bye. Watch.

players.name: Keaon Koloamatangi
players.note: 76-minutes as Koloamatangi's old role as a big minute middle begins to take shape. With a host of FRFs going down in recent weeks, Koloamatangi has added appeal. Worth considering at his current price.

players.name: Xavier Willison
players.note: Bye planning out the window with Xavier Willison failing his HIA overnight. The Broncos' young-gun is now a sell with the 11-day stand down period to rule him out of round 18. He also has a bye in round 19. Patrick Carrigan and Payne Haas are back on deck in round 20.

players.name: Jaydn Su'A
players.note: The Dragons' strike weapon on the right edge crosses for his first try of the season. 57-points in base. Coaches looking to upgrade their 2RF must consider Jaydn Su'A who has started the season on fire. One of the beneficiaries of the new scoring interpretation.

players.name: Jack Williams
players.note: Jack Williams pushed back into the middle forward rotation, playing 75-minutes. Williams is the constant in the Eels forward pack, and is a low-risk low-upside option for the next two major bye rounds.

players.name: Trey Mooney
players.note: Plural - Mooney tries. First career double for the former Raiders' prop. When you thought Mooney might be a sell, he is the gift that keeps on giving. The popular mid-ranger will peak in price right in time for round 12. Huge for patient owners.

players.name: Valentine Holmes
players.note: A late try assist to Tyrell Sloan was the catalyst for Valentine Holmes in tough conditions in Canberra. With the Dragons' attack still trying to click, there is every chance Holmes' hovers around 600k for the remainder of the season.

players.name: Cody Walker
players.note: Two tries in his first two runs as Cody Walker guts the Eels' edge defence. It's perfect timing for Walker owners with the Rabbitohs' playmaker loosing appeal after the Origin period. Hold until round 19.

players.name: Will Penisini
players.note: There is some upside in Will Penisini's value following a solid start to the season. Solid base + evasive stats are creating a pleasing scoring floor. The Eels' centre has also made at least one line break in each of the three matches so far. Slightly under-priced POD.

players.name: Enari Tuala
players.note: The attacking stats have started to flow in recent weeks for Enari Tuala. While maintaining strong base either at centre or on the wing, the attacking output was the only aspect missing. Look for a decent price jump over the coming rounds.

players.name: Kaeo Weekes
players.note: One of the best individual tries of the season. Kaeo Weekes crossed twice and has proved to be a consistent spark in attack. POD play with the Dragons up next.

players.name: Tino Fa'asuamaleaui
players.note: 73-minutes and 72 points in base overnight. Tino Fa'asuamaleaui will plateau in price heading into the final Origin match. With Jackson Ford and Kai Pearce-Paul sidelined long-term, the Titans' captain shuffles up the list of viable options for the run home. Expect Tino to remain a borderline POD in around 19/20.

players.name: Taine Tuaupiki
players.note: The Dragons have allowed 96 points to fullbacks over the last six weeks. Taine Tuaupiki is a POD buy with upside.

players.name: William Warbrick
players.note: The cash grab is well and truely on with Warbrick crossing for his 15th try of the season. Owners will need to wait until at least round 19 to see the full price increase. Typically, Storm wingers will peak quickly following a blip in scoring.

players.name: Tony Sukkar
players.note: Cheapie mode activated as Tony Sukkar crosses for his first career try. With the Tigers' out on their feet, the young backrower earned an early mark with Alex Seyfarth pushing onto the right edge. It's a rotation worth watching when Samuela Fainu returns as soon as next week. Expect Sukkar to retain his starting spot, but whether he plays 80-minutes is a concern.

players.name: Fletcher Sharpe
players.note: The smarts of Fletcher Sharpe were on full display with an effective running game around the ruck in tough conditions on Sunday afternoon. The Knights' five-eighth will be a popular round 18 buy with genuine appeal for the run home.

players.name: Reece Walsh
players.note: Tough conditions for Reece Walsh to leave his mark on the Roosters. The Broncos' found most of their success playing flat and fast on the advantage line, and not out the back of shape. Walsh continues to be a watch given his price, but is well behind the top FB options for the run home.

players.name: Ethan Strange
players.note: Quality by Ethan Strange backing up from Origin. A try and a try assist, and playing both sides of the field at times. Given his strong form, we could see Strange emerge as a top 5/8 for the run home.

players.name: Damien Cook
players.note: The return of Jacob Liddle has ended Damien Cook's electric start to the season. The pair were a direct swap with Cook seeing just 56-minutes. Given the Dragons' pack is at full strength and features numerous workers, Cook & Liddle may remain in a fight for game time.

players.name: Reece Robson
players.note: The most in-form hooker in the game produced his third major attacking stat in as many games. With State of Origin quickly approaching, Robson has little appeal. His form does open up a third top hooker for a run home.

players.name: Dallin Watene-Zelezniak
players.note: Bang on the match up potential with DWZ crossing for two tries. His strong form continues and should be considered for later in the season. The Warriors' home run appears a favourable one at this point in the year.

players.name: Taniela Paseka
players.note: Second wind as Taniela Paseka records his second 70+ point score in three weeks. On this occasion, Paseka found early success with offloading. Long serving owners can hold for at least another week.

players.name: Dylan Brown
players.note: A 60m try highlighted Dylan Brown's attacking upside. When on the field, he has flashed his skillset. He may be a tough add while the Knights' are still finding his feet. POD buy.

players.name: Blaize Talagi
players.note: As good of a rebound game as you can get, as Blaize Talagi produced a try and an assist in round seven. The Panthers' five-eighth has short-term appeal as a cash grab, similar to that of Cody Walker recently.

players.name: Angus Crichton
players.note: Throwback game with Angus Crichton finding his trademark offload (5). If this is a regular thing, the Roosters' backrower will begin to mix it with the top 2RF options.

players.name: Jahream Bula
players.note: A classy return from injury as Jahream Bula crossed for an early try and look dangerous with the ball. Expect Bula to bottom out in price next week and be a POD pick up with the Tigers' next bye not until round 2.

players.name: Setu Tu
players.note: Setu Tu was one of the lone bright spots for the Dragons on Saturday afternoon. He smashed the season average by right wingers against the Roosters, posting a try and two assists. His form may continue against an inconsistent Knights side in round nine.

players.name: Hugo Savala
players.note: Two try assists in a quality bounce back game at Suncorp. Savala's availability as a 5/8 will make him a super POD for the run home. He will bottom out in price by round 19. Must hold his spot at centre.

players.name: Jaxon Purdue
players.note: Second match in the halves, added some much-needed juice to the Cowboys' attack against the Panthers. The role will be short-lived with Tom Dearden set to return in round 19.

players.name: Matthew Dufty
players.note: A 10-minute stint with Jye Gray getting an early mark. Dufty is now a priority sell  who could easily become a AE nightmare over the coming. weeks.

players.name: Kotoni Staggs
players.note: The Broncos' strike weapon is starting to warm with a match winning try against the Storm on Friday night. Three tries to start the season won't be enough to save Staggs' monster breakeven. The draw does lighten up, with all three Queensland teams next up. Expect to see Staggs bottom out in price by round six.

players.name: Kodi Nikorima
players.note: All class from Nikorima who produced a try and two assists in the absence of Isaiya Katoa. The Dolphins' five-eighth flashes plenty of upside in a high octane attack. He is a super POD to finish the Origin period.

players.name: Alofiana Khan-Pereira
players.note: It's not often we get prime match ups this early in the season. The Dragons' have leaked 113 points to left wingers over the last month. AKP could match his absurd breakeven.

players.name: Paul Alamoti
players.note: Three tries in the last two matches will see Paul Alamoti push towards 600k. The forgotten man in the Panthers' backline as shown glimpses of top form. Worth watching for coaches looking to nab an extra Panthers' back.

players.name: Zac Hosking
players.note: Zac Hosking maintains 80-minute role on the right with Simi Sasagi playing centre. Despite the Raiders' round 18 bye, Hosking could be held over the coming weeks, especially with injuries forcing trades - a trend that could continue for some weeks.

players.name: Arama Hau
players.note: Crucial errors and some poor defensive reads resulted in Hau being hooked early in the Titans' match with he Bulldogs. It's the second time he has been hooked this season for fading out of the game early. Owners may be forced to hold until team lists come out in round 19.

players.name: Apisai Koroisau
players.note: Api Koroisau could takeover the goal kicking duties with Adam Doueihi (hamstring) set for an extended stint on the sidelines. Coaches will likely see at least one price drop before needing to make a move.

players.name: Mitchell Barnett
players.note: 59-minutes for Barnett, and almost a try. It appears Mitchell Barnett will be the one to fill the void left by Jackson Ford. Those shoes could be equally shared by Barnett and James Fisher Harris when he returns in a fortnight. Barnett has appeal given his current price.

players.name: Naufahu Whyte
players.note: Shifting to lock, Naufahu Whyte managed 59-minutes in round one. Spencer Leniu is expected to miss time with a hamstring injury which should bode well for Whyte over the coming weeks. His role as a middle forward should resemble 2025.

players.name: Viliame Kikau
players.note: The Bulldogs' left edge found the blueprint to attack on the edges - early ball to Kikau in space, and let him do this thing. First try of the new season for the hulking edge forward. If the Bulldogs are to give this competition a shake, Kikau, and Jacob Preston for that matter will need to be relied on.

players.name: Luciano Leilua
players.note: 65+ in base and a try. It's the third consecutive game Luciano Leilua has played 80-minutes. In the past he has flashed tremendous upside when going the distance. Often it's the lack of game time that has limited Leilua, and not his ability. Looks fitter. Worth considering. Offers early bye coverage.

players.name: Jesse Colquhoun
players.note: We've seen Jesse Colquhoun's game time slowly decrease with Cameron McInnes re-gaining match fitness. Owners are facing a tricky next fortnight with the popular early season cheapie leaking cash, however offers round 18 coverage. Coaches playing H2H can sell, whereas owners playing overall could hold.

players.name: Max Plath
players.note: Max Plath put in his best Origin audition with a gutsy performance through the middle which included a try. the return of Jeremy Marshall-King will push Plath into the forward rotation with the most upside at lock. Should Plath be selected for Origin, he will miss at least four of the next seven weeks.

players.name: Isaiya Katoa
players.note: The key man in the Dolphins' crushing win over the Roosters on Friday night. Katoa crossed for a try and had a hand in five tries. A late finger injury put a dampener on an outstanding showing.

players.name: Campbell Graham
players.note: In two matches in the centres, Campbell Graham has average over 40-points in base stats. If he is to miss NSW selection, Graham could prove to be a high-floor CTW with upside during the Origin period. One to watch.

players.name: Nat Butcher
players.note: 80-minutes on the right edge for Nat Butcher in round one. 65+ in base. Job security is good while Victor Radley is suspended. Solid short-term mid-ranger with plenty of cash to be made.

players.name: Jack Howarth
players.note: Speak of Jack Howarth in the Origin arena may of sparked the talented centre, putting together one of his best performances of the season overnight. Storm centres are notoriously poor for SC, however if Howarth can improve his scoring floor with a higher work-rate as seen over the last fortnight, there could be some hope. The Storm attack also needs to show some consistency.

players.name: Sandon Smith
players.note: A controlling performance by Sandon Smith as the Knights' attack clicks. While Dylan Brown is sidelined, Smith carries some appeal. The Knights experimented with Phoenix Crossland playing a period of the game at lock while Harrison Graham was at hooker. At full strength, this could be Smith's role.

players.name: Moses Leo
players.note: A middle-season resurgence has been halted with Moses Leo leaving the field with a category one HIA. While he won't miss any matches (Storm bye round 18), it's worth upgrading at his current price.

players.name: Jeral Skelton
players.note: Jeral Skelton replaced Taylan May and pushed out to the right wing. 39-points in base, including a try capped a strong performance. Based on May's prognosis, Skelton could re-gain a starting job as soon as round three.

players.name: Jojo Fifita
players.note: Seven tries in his last three matches against the Broncos.

players.name: Jye Gray
players.note: A classy and energetic return from injury saw Jye Gray post a try assist and 36-points in base. Expect Gray to bottom out in price in time for the round 12 bye.

players.name: Jermaine McEwen
players.note: A strong round 10 performance by Jermaine McEwen who bent the line with each carry. It was a powerful bounce back game after a quiet effort against the Rabbitohs. Worth watching for round 18.

players.name: Josh King
players.note: Shades of 2023 as Josh King amounted his highest base stat output ever (74 points). One less forward rotation with an in-game injury to Tyran Wishart provided King with the opportunity longer minutes through the middle. In a Storm side lacking confidence, King hasn't shirked his responsibility.

players.name: Oliver Pascoe
players.note: Sam Verrills enters the chat. For the first time since round 7, Oliver Pascoe had the smaller role in the HOK rotation. Verrills' return and majority share of the game time was purely rotation. It's a hit to Pascoe's prospects for the run home, especially if both hookers stay healthy. Blayke Brailey anyone?

players.name: Grant Anderson
players.note: One of the Broncos' best as Grant Anderson steps in for centre Gehamat Shibasaki. Look for him to hold is spot up until round 18. Anderson has likely bottomed out in price and will offer short-term bye round coverage.

players.name: Roger Tuivasa-Sheck
players.note: Roger Tuivasa-Sheck has found plenty of success at centre over the last month. Two tries and three try assists have catapulted RTS up the rankings. Attacking upside, 33 point average in base. POD appeal at a discounted price.

players.name: Daly Cherry-Evans
players.note: First try and try assist in Roosters colours. Daly Cherry-Evans was the primary playmaker against the Rabbitohs in round two. Likely match-up based, we could see Sam Walker and DCE trade primary roles on a weekly basis. Given the state of the 5/8 position, Cherry-Evans will likely pop numerous times during the season.

players.name: Sunia Turuva
players.note: The Tigers' peppered the Knights' left edge with Sunia Turuva registering 5 line breaks and three tries. The Tigers' winger has short-term appeal with a big cash grab on the horizon. Taylan May could return in the centres in the next 1-2 weeks, boosting Turuva's upside.

players.name: Joey Walsh
players.note: It could be generation next with Luke Brooks going down with a knee injury. Joey Walsh has been put on report for a hip drop. Should he escape suspension, he could be a valuable cheapie for round 18.

players.name: Jesse Ramien
players.note: Third try of the season overnight, and first since round 1. Jesse Ramien's value has slid considerably since the beginning of the season with both base and attacking stats drying up. His base in particular created a nice scoring floor last season - it was the attacking numbers that weren't forthcoming. Ramien at mid-range value is a watch with the Sharks' offering round 18/19 coverage.

players.name: Savelio Tamale
players.note: 10 tackle breaks for Savilio Tamale in round one. The wet conditions created next to no try scoring opportunities. It was pleasing to see that Tamale was able to generate evasive attacking stats, similar to his debut 2025 season. One name to watch if the Raiders' attack clicks on the edges.

players.name: Ronald Volkman
players.note: Some nice touches by Ronald Volkman, including a line break in golden point to set up a Mitchell Moses field goal. Volkman has flashed his talents and has done enough to hold off Jonah Pezet short-term. Volkman shapes as a mid-range HLF with attacking upside. The Eels' round 12 bye will limit buy-ins.

players.name: Sialetili Faeamani
players.note: Backing up last week's breakout performance, Faeamani produced a try great offloading try assist, and try in a 100-point effort. The Titans' flyer posts the highest by a right winger against the Warriors this season. A must-hold through the Titans' round 8 bye with a series of sizeable price rises on the cards.

players.name: Liam Martin
players.note: Liam Martin with his best Origin audition, producing a try and a try assist on Saturday night. Martin sits squarely in mid-range territory, and an inconsistent role on the right edge will likely keep him there.

players.name: Jordan Samrani
players.note: Replacing Will Penisini (groin), Jordan Samrani produced strong base, and a try, and the game-tying try assist. Samrani has flashed plenty of potential when given the opportunity, often playing in some dismal Eel backlines. Now he has a chance in an Eels side beginning to unlock. He is a dual to keep an eye on after the Eels' round 12.

players.name: Tom Gilbert
players.note: A huge base stat effort in humid conditions. Tom Gilbert's work-rate was benefitted by a grinding game with plenty of fatigue. He has put himself in the shop front window for Billy Slater's Origin team in game one.

players.name: Matt Croker
players.note: A gutsy round 14 performance that included 50+ in base and three offloads. An extended role in light of Phoenix Crossland leaving the field with a neck injury in the opening minutes.

players.name: Ethan Roberts
players.note: Solid showing by Ethan Roberts in his NRL on the Tigers' left edge in place of Sam Fainu. With the Tigers' on a bye in round 12, coaches will get a fortnight to decide on the dual cheapie.

players.name: Connor Tracey
players.note: A huge start to 2026 for Connor Tracey. One of the expendable players in the Bulldogs' backline has proven a difference maker in both matches so far. Losing his dual status this season, Tracey will be one of the least owned fullbacks in Supercoach. An improved Bulldogs attack on both edges will help Tracey's game.

players.name: Bronson Xerri
players.note: Best game of the season as Bronson Xerri crushed Nick Meaney. Xerri has flashed upside at times, however the Bulldogs attack has often let him down. One to watch, if he gets more early ball.

players.name: Cameron McInnes
players.note: 74-points in base stats, Cameron McInnes' highest mark since round four 2025. The shackles were cast off overnight as McInnes played big minutes in the Sharks' middle. An anomaly based on the Sharks' most recent forward rotation, it's worth keeping an eye on McInnes and his role heading into round 16. Given his current price, there is plenty of value if his role expands.

players.name: Ethan Sanders
players.note: A late try assist to Daine Laurie overcome an early stint in the sin bin. It was one of Sanders' better performances of the season, finding success attacking the Eels' right edge defence. With the Raiders' on a bye in round 18, owners could now update Sanders to a bye friendly option.

players.name: Moses Suli
players.note: The new scoring system comes through for Moses Suli as the Dragons centre records two try assists. Suli's work-rate has been modest to start the season. The Dragons' tendency to go through the hands on backline moves could assist Suli at times during the season. If he get's more involved, he could have more appeal.

players.name: Reed Mahoney
players.note: Tries against his former clubs in consecutive weeks. The revival of Reed Mahoney is well and truely on with Soni Luki going down with a medial ligament injury. Mid-range value with some upside heading into round 12.

players.name: Thomas Mikaele
players.note: The Cowboys' best forward in their emphatic win over the Bulldogs on Friday night. Thomas Mikaele took on the Bulldogs' forward pack and proved effective in contact, and defence. The Cowboys' forward has short-term appeal as a mid-range FRF with good early Origin bye coverage.

players.name: Brendan Piakura
players.note: Some of the very best of Brendan Piakura overnight with a try and offloading try assist to Tom Duffy - a play he made to Grant Anderson in round 14. Scoring floor and base output has always been a concern for Piakura.

players.name: Ezra Mam
players.note: Third score of 80+ in four weeks without Reece Walsh. Mam lit up a Bulldogs' right side defence that was caught ball watching multiple times. With Walsh set to return next week, Mam's attacking opportunities down the left edge with take a slight hit. The Broncos' five-eighth could pop up as a buy after their round 12 bye.

players.name: Josiah Karapani
players.note: One of a couple of Broncos' standouts as Josiah Karapani bounced back to cross for his fifth try of the season. The Broncos' premiership winger is well under-priced and figures to rebound over the coming weeks. The Broncos' attack will play a pivotal role as to how close Karapani can get to his starting price.

players.name: Tom Duffy
players.note: Tom Duffy steps in for Adam Reynolds, impressing with a try assist and a solid on the ball display. The Broncos' captain left the field with a category one HIA, paving the way for Duffy to start next week and become a low-range cheapie. Will goal kick over Reece Walsh.

players.name: Fletcher Hunt
players.note: AE nightmare with Fletcher Hunt playing the final two minutes on Saturday night. Priority sell.

players.name: Cooper Clarke
players.note: Back in the middle forward rotation, Cooper Clarke was best forward on ground for the Storm. Routinely bending the line backwards and generating ruck speed. Owners who have held through necessity can continue to hold heading into Magic Round.

players.name: Moeaki Fotuaika
players.note: A career-high in base stats (78) as Moeaki Fotuaika turns his season around with the captaincy role. Of all the mid-range FRF options, Fotuaika could have the most upside. More appeal in draft, however given his current price, he is worth considering.

players.name: Adam Reynolds
players.note: A 20-minute blitz that included two try assists yielded Reynolds 80% of his scoring. The Broncos' attack sauntered during the middle part of the game, before Reynolds succumbed to a rip complaint late. Reynolds' injury history will make him a tough play all season.

players.name: Adam Pompey
players.note: Adam Pompey could shape as a super POD in round 15 with the added goal kicking duties, and taking over from Roger Tuivasa-Sheck. Attacking upside is the only question, but in a firing Warriors side, those numbers should come.

players.name: Briton Nikora
players.note: The run on Briton Nikora is on with the gun forward crossing for his second try of the season. Origin clouds his buy-now status, but at sub 400k he is hard to resist. QLD appearance could spark him into form.

players.name: Leka Halasima
players.note: A dip in production as the Warriors' edges saw little opportunity in loss to the Tigers. A HIA and a return to the field at centre didn't help Halasima's cause. We saw Beau Fermor make the most of a 2RF/CTW role last season - Halasima could be the next, if he can improve his base. work-rate. He will have added appeal if he earns dual status in round six.

players.name: Corey Jensen
players.note: A work-horse effort coming back from concussion in round one. Corey Jensen produced 50+ in base in a 54-minute display against the Eels overnight. While lacking the attacking upside of some other mid-range options, he is one who should still benefit under the new scoring system. Keep Jensen in your back pocket until after the Storm game in round 3.

players.name: Loko Pasifiki Tonga
players.note: A steady increase in game time, and work-rate sees Loko Pasifiki Tonga as the next mid-range FRF with upside. Round 16 he is a low priority buy. Attacking stats could elevate his game as per numerous FRFs in round 14.

players.name: Jason Saab
players.note: Cash in on Saab? The Sea Eagles' winger a boom or bust option in a favourable match up against the Titans who have leaked 58 points on average to the last four right wingers.

players.name: Morgan Knowles
players.note: 68-minutes and 57 points in base stats. A typical Morgan Knowles performance in Dolphins regular season debut. His role in the middle forward rotation resembles his time at St Helens. Expect a high base work-rate and some short passes at the line. One of a handful of mid-range forwards to out perform their starting price in round one.

players.name: Nick Meaney
players.note: Two try effort on the left wing will ensure Nick Meaney will bottom out in price. There is too much value here to completely ignore. Some may baulk at Meaney while the Storm continue their slide.

players.name: Connor Watson
players.note: Replacing Egan Butcher at lock, Connor Watson was one of the Roosters' best with quality leg speed around the ruck and strong defence. We have just seen Watson's best position with Reece Robson returning to a full-time hooker role. Game time and opportunity is key.

players.name: Joe Chan
players.note: 18-minutes off the bench. Joe Chan has lost his starting spot to edge forwards Ativalu Lisati and Shawn Blore. Chan is a priority sell.

players.name: Jock Madden
players.note: Jock Madden quickly bottoms out in price with some nice touches against the Sea Eagles. While his combination with Jahrome Luai is a work-in-progess, Madden's style of play should bode well for the Tigers' without Adam Doueihi. Madden will have short-term appeal following he round 12 bye.

players.name: Sam McIntyre
players.note: Cowboys' Mr Fix-it found himself at left back row, left centre and right centre. Despite the disruption, McIntyre was one of the Cowboys' best. Job security is questionable. Look for Kai O'Donnell to start to eat into his game time on the edge, and eventually be replaced by Jeremiah Nanai when he returns from injury in April.

players.name: Sam Stonestreet
players.note: 34-points in base stats and a line break. Sam Stonestreet has showcased his speed through the opening rounds, albeit without a try. Owners could downgrade to a cheapie or upgrade to a Stephen Crichton or Latrell Mitchell.

players.name: Klese Haas
players.note: A career-high in offloads (5) as Klese Haas helped get the Titans' attack moving overnight. Accumulative attacking numbers are not something you associate with Haas, but he certainly has it in his game. An increase in game time over the last 2-3 matches will provide ample opportunity. Mid-range watch.

players.name: Patrick Herbert
players.note: 63-minutes at centre. Replaced by Jock Madden to pushed Adam Doueihi out into the centres. Just when you thought you could nab another Tigers' cheapie, Benji Marshall throws up a curveball. Patrick Herbert is a genuine cheapie, however last night's rotation will make things tricky.

players.name: Jonah Pezet
players.note: A HIA and hamstring injury ended Jonah Pezet's evening after a kick-happy start. The popular five-eighth is now a sell with an extended stint on the sidelines expected.

players.name: Cooper Bai
players.note: Bai (HIA) played just 11-minutes in round 16. The Titans' lock will miss the next two matches through stand down and byes.

players.name: Sione Fainu
players.note: Two try effort by Sione Fainu on the Tigers' left edge. Look for Fainu to hold the left backrow spot until Samuela Fainu returns from injury in round 19/20.

players.name: Zane Harrison
players.note: Breakout performance as Zane Harrison produced three try assists and the match-winning field goal against the Panthers on Saturday night. Limited appeal in classic with a Titans' bye in round 18. Could be held on the bench in draft.

players.name: Jesse Arthars
players.note: Rock-solid under the high ball, and a try on the stroke of halftime give a great indication into Jesse Arthars role and job security over the next month. The Broncos stand-in fullback is now a SC buy with plenty of upside over the coming weeks.

players.name: Jacob Laban
players.note: Season high in base (55), plus a try. Jacob Laban's increased role on the edge is quickly pushing him past mid-range territory. Limited appeal for the run home - has more appeal in draft.

players.name: Christian Tuipulotu
players.note: A late try will stop the slide as Tuipulotu will appreciate in price for the first time this season. If it wasn't for the inconsistent Dragons' attack, Tuipulotu would attract much more interest. He is one to keep an eye on if the Dragons' left edge can click.

players.name: Jake Simpkin
players.note: 53-minutes for Jake Simpkin in round two. A direct swap with Brandon Wakeham. This should be a standard bench rotation with no in-game injuries. Simpkin's value slips.

players.name: Ben Talty
players.note: In the absence of the three starting middles for the Broncos, Ben Talty stood up again, producing over 60 in base in a 53-minute effort. Whilst Payne Haas and Corey Jensen are sideline, Talty has mid-range appeal that could extend into the Origin period.

players.name: Joash Papalii
players.note: Best performance in Eels colours at fullback amounting a try and an assist. Papalii's speed and footwork on the edges caused the Cowboys issues as the Eels' attack found some much needed cohesion and confidence. Papalii remains a mid-range 5/8 with upside. No round 12 coverage will hurt buy rates.

players.name: Manaia Waitere
players.note: First career try and a 42-point base stat effort in the centres. Should Moses Leo miss extra time, Manaia Waitere could be a downgrade option.

players.name: Tallyn Da Silva
players.note: 80-minutes in the absence of Ryley Smith, Da Silva impressed with a try, two line breaks and 31-points in base. While Smith will come back into the team either side of the Eels round 12 bye, Da Silva is one to monitor if he can lockdown a big-minute role. Attacking upside.

players.name: Bailey Hayward
players.note: Run Bailey Run. The Bulldogs hooker doubles his run numbers in the Good Friday loss to the Bunnies. All banter aside, Hayward is a middling HOK option who projects to founder around 350-400k without an injection of attacking stats.

players.name: Jackson Shereb
players.note: 60-minutes for Jackson Shereb, including a HIA. First price rise on the cards in round 13. Haumalo Olakau'atu is expected to return from Origin commitments, pushing Shereb back to the bench. Low priority buy with no round 15 coverage.

players.name: Saxon Pryke
players.note: A dip in game time after last multiple crucial errors last week. Saxon Pryke remains entrenched on the Eels bench in a re-worked middle forward rotation that now includes Jack Williams. SC value is low. Pryke is a slow-burn forward who could peak around the end of the Origin period.

players.name: Kobe Hetherington
players.note: A head knock to Jake Trbojevic resulted in an increase of game time (74-minutes) in round four. 63-points in base. It's a performance to note, especially if Kobe Hetherington eventually secures a big-minute role in a forward pack who needs a leader.

players.name: Thomas Flegler
players.note: 42-minutes in round seven. Work-rate and opportunity continues to limit Tom Flegler's upside. Unless starting, Flegler isn't a consideration.

players.name: Jonathan Sua
players.note: Jonathan Sua has one for week to fill a job for owners. The popular cheapie has struggled with errors in his first NRL experience. As the Bulldogs' outside backs begin to get healthier, Sua's days could be numbered.

players.name: Cody Ramsey
players.note: A denied try in the opening minutes set the tone for Ramsey and the rest of the Roosters' attack in wet conditions. He may hold his spot in round 16 if the Roosters' choose to rest James Tedesco following Origin.

players.name: Brandon Smith
players.note: Season-high in game time as Brandon Smith played exclusively at hooker. Finally healthy, Smith has appeal heading into round 18. Has mid-range upside given his role and price.

players.name: Heamasi Makasini
players.note: A night to forget for Heamasi Makasini. Ignoring the crucial errors, Makasini's pre-season form has yet to be seen in first grade. He can be left in the reserves moving forward.

players.name: Benaiah Ioelu
players.note: 25-minutes in round two for Benaiah Ioelu. Not exactly what owners wanted as Connor Watson saw his game time increase to 55-minutes at hooker. While he has flashed attacking potential, Ioelu's inconsistent role makes him a sell/upgrade heading into round three.

players.name: Siosifa Talakai
players.note: Shades of 2022 a Sifa Talakai crossed for two first half tries and his first 100-point performance since 2023. Unfortunately for coaches Sione Katoa and Jesse Ramien are due back from next week. The Talakai/ CTW cheapie pipe dream could be just that.

players.name: Tyrell Sloan
players.note: Tyrell Sloan is playing for a new contact. Last night's performance was the most amount of runs (24) he has made in a match. While it was a up and down showing at fullback, Sloan did flash his creative skill set. Short-term appeal, just behind Jesse Arthars.

players.name: Daniel Atkinson
players.note: The try drought is over as Daniel Atkinson crossed for his first points in Dragons' colours. Long-serving owners will be rejoicing in what is shaping as a tough H2H week for coaches. If the Atkinson scores next week, and the Dragons win, we might be onto something.

players.name: Hohepa Puru
players.note: Hohepa Puru has entered a HOK time share with Jayden Barrell in recent weeks. While a cheapie, his value is limited and will dry up quickly once Blakye Brailey returns from a fractured arm.

players.name: Tristan Hope
players.note: Strong base by Tristan Hope who went the distance filling-in for the suspended Api Koroisau. Barring injury, Hope will play another two matches for the Tigers' captain returns. Not a buy.

players.name: Francis Manuleleua
players.note: Auto Emergency nightmare as Francis Mannuleleua takes the field for the final 8-minutes. Owners will enjoy a small price rise this week. Moving forward, his role on the bench is dicey for owners.

players.name: Faaletino Tavana
players.note: Second consecutive try as Faaletino Tavana emerges as a popular cheapie heading into round six. Jock Madden's high ball target, Tavana gained success in his first attempt jumping over Josh Addo-Carr. The Tigers' winger will be try dependent, however coaches are at the stage where they are begging for CTW downgrade options.

players.name: Gehamat Shibasaki
players.note: Replacin Grant Anderson (knee), Gehamat Shibasaki made an instant impact on the left edge with improved defensive reads and a try. The 57-minute stint was a far-cry from the opening two rounds where he was asleep at the wheel. A huge price drop is on the cards over the next 2-3 weeks. He is one to watch however, especially this is a wake up call.

players.name: Sam Verrills
players.note: A mixed day for Sam Verrills who struggled with his passing, but bounced back strong with the Titans first try of the season. He would leave the field after 63-minutes with a shoulder complaint. Former rake Chris Randall slotted into hooker in his absence. There is every chance this was part of the rotation despite the injury. Worth watching Randall's role in round 2.

players.name: Antonio Verhoeven
players.note: First career try for Antonio Verhoeven, crossing off a Reece Walsh cut-out ball. With a host of injured Broncos' opportunity has presented itself to the gun rookie winger. Deine Mariner is sidelined long-term opening up a right wing spot. While Jesse Arthars will likely feature there at some point, Verhoeven is a cheapie worth owning with the Origin period nearing.

players.name: Jack Underhill
players.note: Plenty to like about Jack Underhill who has provided plenty of energy off the bench in his first two NRL matches. He firms as a low-grade FRF cheapie heading into round 13. Underhill is level with Saxon Pryke for role and output off the bench.

players.name: J'maine Hopgood
players.note: A hip drop tackle ended J'maine Hopgood's afternoon. A season-ending ACL injury will result in owners selling the Eels prop.

players.name: Toni Mataele
players.note: Solid impact off the bench in back-to-back games. Toni Mataele is filling the void left by injured stars on the Eels bench. Given his PPM, Mataele could pop if he earns close to 40-minutes in game time.

players.name: Soni Luke
players.note: Reed Mahoney owners are looking at Soni Luke in disgust. Replacing the former Bulldog, Luke added some much needed juice out of dummy half, produce a try and several probing runs. There is every chance the Cowboys' HOK rotation enters into a genuine equal time share.

players.name: Hugo Peel
players.note: Remained on the right wing despite Sua Fa'alogo leaving the field with a HIA. Peel has seen little attacking opportunities so far. He has limited appeal as a cheapie at this time.

players.name: Alekolasimi Jones
players.note: Passed the eye test in his NRL debut. 'AJ' Jones could find a home in a Bulldogs' forward pack lacking some substance. One to watch if he can receive more game time.

players.name: Kade Reed
players.note: A tough afternoon for Kade Reed to struggled to create opportunities, not through lack of trying. Zero SC appeal at this point in time. Reed may benefit from having a hard running edge forward to straighten the attack. Hamish Stewart or Jaydn Su'a.

players.name: Angus Hinchey
players.note: First career goal kick for Angus Hinchey. He moves up the pecking order at Melbourne, now fifth choice option.
